name: Fried Pork Pies (Festival)
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
1. Mix oil and flour in a 10-inch skillet.
2. Add ground chuck, ground pork, onion, green onion, parsley, salt, black pepper, red pepper, and oregano to the pan.
3. Cook until meat is no longer pink.
4. Add seasonings (salt, black pepper, red pepper, and oregano) to taste.
5. Drain excess oil from the cooked mixture.
6. Cool before placing filling in pastry dough.
Pastry Dough:
1. Sift flour, salt, and baking powder into a 1-quart bowl.
2. Mix well with shortening until crumbly.
3. Add milk and beaten eggs to the flour mixture and mix until smooth.
4. Divide dough into 10 portions.
5. Place one portion on a floured board and roll into a circle approximately 7 inches in diameter.
6. Use a lid or saucer to cut into a circle about 6 inches in diameter.
7. Place several tablespoonsful of the meat mixture on one edge of the pastry.
8. Fold dough over, dampen edges with water, and crimp together with a fork.
9. Preheat oil to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) in a deep fat electric fryer.
10. Place pies in oil and fry until golden brown.
11. Remove from oil and drain on absorbent paper.
